title: Proteus verse — Tot tibi sunt dotes
verse: Tot tibi sunt dotes, Virgo, quot sidera caelo
translation: As many graces are yours, O Virgin, as there are stars in the sky
device: versus proteus (proteus verse): the words may be reordered, and each admitted order still scans as a dactylic hexameter and keeps the sense
attribution: Bernardus Bauhusius (Bernard van Bauhuijsen), early 17th c.; famously counted and debated by Erycius Puteanus and treated in Caramuel's Primus Calamus, which is centrally concerned with this permutational class of verse.
classical_counts_note: Period hand-counts vary by scansion rule and by whether sense must be preserved (Puteanus reported 1022 valid permutations). The 'true' number is rule-dependent, which is the point: the count is a function of the meter gate.
chiron_note: 8 words => 8! = 40320 orderings before the meter gate. With a strict scanner (CLTK, or a native Rust kernel) Chiron filters to the metrically-valid set; with the coarse pure-Python fallback it reports an upper bound. Reproduce: python3 primus_verses.py --proteus "Tot tibi sunt dotes Virgo quot sidera caelo" --max 40320
provenance: Curated by hand from scholarship. NOT OCR-extracted from the 1663 scan.
